title: Dynamic Menu Items

Dynamic Menu Items

Dynamic Menu Items

Dynamic menus contain one or more menu items that are generated at runtime, and inserted in a predetermined location of a drop-down or popup menu. They can be used, for example, to display and open recently accessed objects, list and switch between currently open windows, or provide a pop-up menu that is related to the value of a field.

To enable dynamic menus, you need to:

  • Define the location where the dynamic menu will be inserted by adding a Dynamic Menu item to a drop-down and popup menu. This serves as a placeholder for the dynamic menu.

  • Generate the content of the dynamic menu by defining a nested associative list of one or more menu options. Each menu option must have an identifier, which can be used in the option trigger to define its behavior, and a sublist of properties that define the type of item, the item text, and other optional properties.

  • Insert the content list at the placeholder location using the $inlinemenu ProcScript function in the preDisplay trigger of the placeholder.

  • Define the action performed by each option in the dynamic menu by writing code in the option trigger of the placeholder.

The following illustration shows how menu items are inserted at the dynamic menu location.

Dynamic menus can contain menu options, separators, included menus, and cascaded menus. Included and cascaded menus in dynamic menus can reference static drop-down menu, but not popup menus.

A cascaded menu can reference an existing drop-down menu or it can include another dynamic menu. This makes it possible to nest dynamic menus (although this should be implemented with caution, since it is possible to create menu loops).

Dynamic menus do not have their own item text, nor can they have an accelerator or hint text. However, you can define these for the menu items inserted in the dynamic menu.

Related Links
  • $inlinemenu

    Insert or retrieve one or more menu items at the location of a dynamic menu placeholder

  • trigger preDisplay

    System trigger that is executed before a menu or menu item is displayed.

  • Option: Copy Description and Comments

    Determines whether the new implementation should use the description and comments defined in the component's specification.

  • trigger option

    Trigger for handling the processing that should occur after a menu item is selected.
